title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary:
1. **Add a runtime validation check for `Database` mutual exclusivity** in the webhook's `validate()` function — `Cache` already has both a CEL rule and a defense-in-depth runtime guard, but `Database` only has the CEL rule, leaving a gap if CRD admission is bypassed. Add the matching `if` check and corresponding tests.

2. **Fix the `//go:generate` directive** to also generate webhook manifests (not just CRDs), and align the output flag syntax with the Makefile's canonical `output:crd:artifacts:config=` form — or add a comment explaining that `go generate` is intentionally CRD-only and `make manifests` is the canonical command.

code_comments.body:
**Database XOR constraint missing webhook defense-in-depth**

`Cache` has both a `+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation` CEL rule and a matching runtime guard in `validate()` (the `REQ-009` block, lines 102–110). `Database` has the same CEL XOR constraint:

```go
// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="has(self.clusterRef) != has(self.host)",...
Database commonv1.DatabaseSpec `json:"database"`
```

but no corresponding defense-in-depth check exists in `validate()`. If CRD admission is bypassed (direct `etcdctl` write, admission controller misconfiguration, or the webhook being unavailable during an upgrade), a `Keystone` with both `database.clusterRef` and `database.host` set is accepted silently. The reconciler would then receive an ambiguous database configuration with no in-cluster safeguard.

The project's own review pattern (`apply-defense-in-depth-checks-consistently-to-all-similarly-marked-fields`) requires this: _"An established defense-in-depth pattern applied to some fields but not others creates a false sense of security."_

Add the mirror check immediately after the cache guard, following the same pattern:

```go
// REQ-XXX (CC-0011): Defense-in-depth database mutual-exclusivity check alongside the
// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation CEL rule on KeystoneSpec.Database.
if (k.Spec.Database.ClusterRef != nil) == (k.Spec.Database.Host != "") {
    allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(
        specPath.Child("database"),
        k.Spec.Database,
        "exactly one of clusterRef or host must be set",
    ))
}
```

A corresponding test (mirroring `TestValidate_CacheBothClusterRefAndServersRejected` and `TestValidate_CacheNeitherClusterRefNorServersRejected`) should also be added to `keystone_webhook_test.go`.

The reconciler would then receive an ambiguous database configuration with no in-cluster safeguard.

`````markdown
The project's own review pattern (`apply-defense-in-depth-checks-consistently-to-all-similarly-marked-fields`) requires this: _"An established defense-in-depth pattern applied to some fields but not others creates a false sense of security."_

// REQ-XXX (CC-0011): Defense-in-depth database mutual-exclusivity check alongside the
`````

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added REQ-010 defense-in-depth database mutual-exclusivity check at keystone_webhook.go:112-120, using the same XOR pattern as the cache check: `if (k.Spec.Database.ClusterRef != nil) == (k.Spec.Database.Host != "")`. Added two corresponding tests in keystone_webhook_test.go: TestValidate_DatabaseBothClusterRefAndHostRejected and TestValidate_DatabaseNeitherClusterRefNorHostRejected, mirroring the cache test structure.

code_comments.body:
**`//go:generate` CRD directive skips webhook manifest generation**

The `//go:generate` directive here regenerates the CRD YAML but does not generate the webhook configuration YAML. The `make manifests` target runs two controller-gen commands — one for CRD, one for webhook — but `go generate ./...` from within this package only runs the CRD command. A developer who runs `go generate` instead of `make manifests` will have an up-to-date CRD YAML but a stale (or absent) `config/webhook/` directory.

Additionally, `output:crd:dir=<path>` is an older shorthand; the Makefile uses the canonical `output:crd:artifacts:config=<path>` form. Aligning the directive keeps both regeneration paths consistent:

```go
//go:generate controller-gen object:headerFile=../../../../hack/boilerplate.go.txt paths=.
//go:generate controller-gen crd paths=. output:crd:artifacts:config=../../config/crd/bases
//go:generate controller-gen webhook paths=. output:webhook:artifacts:config=../../config/webhook
```

Or, add a comment clarifying that `make manifests` is the canonical regeneration command and `go generate` is intentionally limited to CRD-only output.

`````markdown
Line: 9

The `make manifests` target runs two controller-gen commands — one for CRD, one for webhook — but `go generate ./...` from within this package only runs the CRD command. A developer who runs `go generate` instead of `make manifests` will have an up-to-date CRD YAML but a stale (or absent) `config/webhook/` directory.

Additionally, `output:crd:dir=<path>` is an older shorthand; the Makefile uses the canonical `output:crd:artifacts:config=<path>` form. Aligning the directive keeps both regeneration paths consistent:

```go
//go:generate controller-gen object:headerFile=../../../../hack/boilerplate.go.txt paths=.
//go:generate controller-gen crd paths=. output:crd:artifacts:config=../../config/crd/bases
//go:generate controller-gen webhook paths=. output:webhook:artifacts:config=../../config/webhook
```

Or, add a comment clarifying that `make manifests` is the canonical regeneration command and `go generate` is intentionally limited to CRD-only output.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Updated groupversion_info.go:8-10 with three //go:generate directives: (1) object generation with headerFile unchanged, (2) CRD generation using the canonical `output:crd:artifacts:config=../../config/crd/bases` form (replacing the older `output:crd:dir=` shorthand), and (3) a new webhook manifest generation directive `controller-gen webhook paths=. output:webhook:artifacts:config=../../config/webhook`. The Makefile manifests target was also updated to run both CRD and webhook controller-gen commands, ensuring `make manifests` and `go generate` are now consistent.

resolution_summary: Both review comments were fully addressed. Comment [0] requested a defense-in-depth runtime check for the Database XOR constraint in validate(), mirroring the existing Cache pattern. The check was added at lines 112-120 of keystone_webhook.go with REQ-010 tracking, and corresponding tests (TestValidate_DatabaseBothClusterRefAndHostRejected, TestValidate_DatabaseNeitherClusterRefNorHostRejected) were added to keystone_webhook_test.go. Comment [1] requested webhook manifest generation be added to the //go:generate directives. The directive was updated to use the canonical output:crd:artifacts:config= form and a new webhook generation directive was added at line 10 of groupversion_info.go.
